title: Support export DPI calls with timing controls
body:
> Thanks for taking the time to report this.

> Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

dpi_test.v:
```
module dpi_test ();

reg rtl_clk;
initial begin
    rtl_clk = 1'b0;
    forever
        #2 rtl_clk = ~rtl_clk;
end

    export "DPI-C" v_export = task dpi_export;
    task dpi_export(input int unsigned i);
        //@(posedge rtl_clk); // comment this out for the task to be executed
        $display("%t: v_export: i=%3d", $time, i);
    endtask

    import "DPI-C" context task dpi_import(input int unsigned n);

    integer n;
initial begin
        $display("Dumping waveforms");
        $dumpfile("waves.fst");
        repeat (10) @(posedge rtl_clk);
        for (n = 3; n < 6; n = n + 1) begin
            $display("%t: calling dpi_import: n =%3d", $time, n);
            dpi_import(n);
        end
        $finish;
end
endmodule
```
test.c
```
#include <assert.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <svdpi.h>

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

extern void v_export (unsigned int i);

int dpi_import(unsigned int len) {
    printf("dpi_import: len=%d\n", len);
    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
        printf("callling v_export: i=%d\n", i);
        v_export(i);
    }
    return 0;
}

#ifdef __cplusplus
}
#endif
```
Compiled using:
```
verilator --binary --trace-fst dpi_test.v test.c
obj_dir/Vdpi_test
```

Yields:
```
Dumping waveforms
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  3
dpi_import: len=3
callling v_export: i=0
                  38: v_export: i=  0
callling v_export: i=1
                  38: v_export: i=  1
callling v_export: i=2
                  38: v_export: i=  2
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  4
dpi_import: len=4
callling v_export: i=0
                  38: v_export: i=  0
callling v_export: i=1
                  38: v_export: i=  1
callling v_export: i=2
                  38: v_export: i=  2
callling v_export: i=3
                  38: v_export: i=  3
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  5
dpi_import: len=5
callling v_export: i=0
                  38: v_export: i=  0
callling v_export: i=1
                  38: v_export: i=  1
callling v_export: i=2
                  38: v_export: i=  2
callling v_export: i=3
                  38: v_export: i=  3
callling v_export: i=4
                  38: v_export: i=  4
- dpi_test.v:27: Verilog $finish
```

Which is fine.

Uncommenting out dpi_test.v:12 (event delay) and rerunning, yields:
```
Dumping waveforms
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  3
dpi_import: len=3
callling v_export: i=0
callling v_export: i=1
callling v_export: i=2
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  4
dpi_import: len=4
callling v_export: i=0
callling v_export: i=1
callling v_export: i=2
callling v_export: i=3
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  5
dpi_import: len=5
callling v_export: i=0
callling v_export: i=1
callling v_export: i=2
callling v_export: i=3
callling v_export: i=4
- dpi_test.v:27: Verilog $finish
```

Running the same code with a Big-3 simulator yields the correct result:
```
Dumping waveforms
                  38: calling dpi_import: n =  3
dpi_import: len=3
callling v_export: i=0
                  42: v_export: i=  0
callling v_export: i=1
                  46: v_export: i=  1
callling v_export: i=2
                  50: v_export: i=  2
                  50: calling dpi_import: n =  4
dpi_import: len=4
callling v_export: i=0
                  54: v_export: i=  0
callling v_export: i=1
                  58: v_export: i=  1
callling v_export: i=2
                  62: v_export: i=  2
callling v_export: i=3
                  66: v_export: i=  3
                  66: calling dpi_import: n =  5
dpi_import: len=5
callling v_export: i=0
                  70: v_export: i=  0
callling v_export: i=1
                  74: v_export: i=  1
callling v_export: i=2
                  78: v_export: i=  2
callling v_export: i=3
                  82: v_export: i=  3
callling v_export: i=4
                  86: v_export: i=  4
Simulation complete via $finish(1) at time 86 NS + 0
./dpi_test.v:27         $finish;
```

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

Verilator 5.011 devel rev v5.010-1-g3e986517c
This is a very recent version although not the current head.

> What OS and distribution are you using?

Ubuntu 20.04

> May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Given a hint I could try, although this doesn't sound simple.
